q: How often (you/go) to the cinema?
a: How often do you go to the cinema?

q: Ben (try) to find a job, but he hasn't had any luck yet.
a: Ben is trying to find a job, but he hasn't had any luck yet.

q: Martina (phone) her mother every day.
a: Martina phones her mother every day.

q: The moon (go) round the earth in about 27 days.
a: The moon goes round the earth in about 27 days.

q: Can you hear those people? What (they/talk) about?
a: Can you hear those people? What are they talking about?

q: I must go now. It (get) late.
a: I must go now. It is getting late.

q: 'Come on! It's time to leave.' 'OK, I (come).'
a: 'Come on! It's time to leave.' 'OK, I'm coming.'

q: Paul is never late. He (always/start) work on time.
a: Paul is never late. He always starts work on time.

q: They don't get on well. They (always/argue).
a: They don't get on well. They're always arguing.

q: I (get) hungry. Let's go and eat something.
a: I'm getting hungry. Let's go and eat something.

q: (you/listen) to the radio? No, you can turn it off.
a: Are you listening to the radio? No, you can turn it off.

q: (you/listen) to the radio a lot? No, not very often.
a: Do you listen to the radio a lot? No, not very often.

q: The River Nile (flow) into the Mediterranean.
a: The River Nile flows into the Mediterranean.

q: The river (flow) very fast today - much faster than usual.
a: The river is flowing very fast today - much faster than usual.

q: I'm not very active. I (not/do) any sport.
a: I'm not very active. I don't do any sport.

q: What (you/usually/do) at weekends?
a: What do you usually do at weekends?

q: Rachel is in New York right now. She (stay) at the Park Hotel.
a: Rachel is in New York right now. She is staying at the Park Hotel.

q: She (always/stay) at the Park Hotel when she's in New York.
a: She always stays at the Park Hotel when she's in New York.

q: Julia is good at languages. She (speak) four languages very well.
a: Julia is good at languages. She speaks four languages very well.

q: Are you ready yet? Everybody (wait) for you.
a: Are you ready yet? Everybody is waiting for you.

q: I've never heard this word. How (you/pronounce) it?
a: I've never heard this word. How do you pronounce it?

q: Kate (not/work) this week. She's on holiday.
a: Kate isn't working this week. She's on holiday.

q: I think my English (improve) slowly. It's better than it was.
a: I think my English is improving slowly. It's better than it was.

q: Nicola (live) in Manchester. She has never lived anywhere else.
a: Nicola lives in Manchester. She has never lived anywhere else.

q: Can we stop walking soon? I (start) to get tired.
a: Can we stop walking soon? I'm starting to get tired.

q: Sam and Tina are in Madrid right now. They (visit) a friend of theirs.
a: Sam and Tina are in Madrid right now. They are visiting a friend of theirs.

q: What (your father/do)? He's an architect.
a: What does your father do? He's an architect.

q: It took me an hour to get to work this morning. Most days (it/not/take) so long.
a: It took me an hour to get to work this morning. Most days it doesn't take so long.

q: I (learn) to drive. My father (teach) me.
a: I'm learning to drive. My father is teaching me.

q: The car has broken down again. That car is useless. It (always/break/down).
a: The car has broken down again. That car is useless. It's always breaking down.

q: Look! You've made the same mistake again. Oh no, not again! I (always/make/the same mistake).
a: Look! You've made the same mistake again. Oh no, not again! I'm always making the same mistake.

q: Oh, I've left my phone at home again. Typical! You (always/leave/your phone at home).
a: Oh, I've left my phone at home again. Typical! You're always leaving your phone at home.
